Before Pickup Checklist

Everything to prepare before you drive to Italy to bring him home.

Documents from breeder

Pack for pickup day

Prepare at home

After entering Switzerland

Important Deadlines at a Glance

Before pickup

Get all documents, prepare home, pack for the trip.

Travel day

Declare at customs, pay VAT if applicable, keep receipt.

Within 10 days

Vet visit, microchip verification, AMICUS and municipality.

Within 1 month

Dog tax, insurance, daily routine, puppy school.

2–3 months

Continue training, socialization and Zürich course requirements if applicable.

Legal Import: Italy → Zürich

Before leaving Italy

Make sure the puppy has an EU Pet Passport, ISO microchip, purchase invoice and any required puppy declaration.

At Swiss customs

Use the red “Goods to Declare” lane. Show documents and pay Swiss VAT if required. Keep the receipt.

After arrival

Visit a Swiss vet within 10 days. Register in AMICUS and with your municipality / Hundekontrolle.

Feeding Guide

AgeMeals/dayNotes
8–12 weeks3–4Keep breeder food first. Change slowly over 7–10 days.
3–6 months3Stable routine, adjust portions with growth and vet advice.
6–12 months2Move toward junior/adult routine.

Training Guide

First weeks
  • Name recognition
  • Toilet routine after sleep, food and play
  • Crate comfort
  • Gentle handling practice
Basic commands
  • Sit, down, come, stay, leave it, drop it
  • Keep sessions 3–5 minutes
  • Reward with tiny treats and praise
GSP focus
  • Recall training is priority number one
  • Loose-leash walking early
  • Sniff games and puzzle toys for mental exercise

Health, Bath & Foods to Avoid

Bath routine

Use puppy-safe dog shampoo every 4–8 weeks or when dirty. Do not over-bathe.

Grooming

Brush weekly, check ears, paws and nails regularly.

Never feed

Chocolate, grapes, raisins, onions, garlic, alcohol, caffeine, xylitol, cooked bones.

🌍 Socialization Checklist

Expose your puppy to new people, environments, sounds and situations gradually and positively.
